name: skill-creator
summary: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill...
description: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
author: openai
value_statement: Creating specialized AI agents requires a structured approach to knowledge management. This skill provides a complete framework for building, validating, and packaging modular skills that extend AI capabilities with domain-specific workflows, tools, and resources.
actual_capabilities: Initialize new skill directories with SKILL.md templates and resource folders
Validate skill structure, naming conventions, and YAML frontmatter requirements
Package skills into distributable .skill files using ZIP compression
Apply progressive disclosure design patterns for efficient context management
Organize bundled resources (scripts, references, assets) for optimal AI access

use_cases.title: Automate repetitive coding tasks
use_cases.description: Create skills that bundle workflow scripts, boilerplate templates, and domain-specific coding patterns for consistent code generation.

use_cases.title: Standardize team practices
use_cases.description: Build skills that encode company coding standards, review checklists, and deployment procedures for consistent team workflows.

use_cases.title: Share specialized knowledge
use_cases.description: Package domain expertise (schemas, APIs, business logic) into skills that help AI agents handle specialized queries accurately.

prompt_templates.title: Create basic skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new skill called [skill-name] in [output-path] with resources for scripts and references.

prompt_templates.title: Add skill resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Add [scripts|references|assets] directories to the [skill-name] skill and include example files.

prompt_templates.title: Validate skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Run validation on [skill-path] to check if the skill meets all requirements for packaging.

prompt_templates.title: Package skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Package the skill at [skill-path] into a .skill file and save it to [output-directory].

limitations: This skill provides guidance only; it does not execute on behalf of users
Skills are triggered by metadata (name + description), not by file extensions or paths
User must provide all domain expertise and content for their skills

faq.question: Which platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: The skill creator is designed for Codex but skills created work on Codex, Claude, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What are the limits for skill names?
faq.answer: Skill names must be 64 characters or less using only lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens.

faq.question: How do I integrate skills with existing projects?
faq.answer: Skills are standalone packages. Place them in your skills directory and they activate based on name and description matching.

faq.question: Is my data safe when creating skills?
faq.answer: Yes. Skill creation scripts run locally, read and write only specified directories, and do not access network or environment variables.

faq.question: Why does validation fail on my skill?
faq.answer: Common issues: missing frontmatter, invalid YAML, disallowed keys in metadata, name with uppercase or special characters, or description over 1024 characters.

faq.question: How is this different from Claude projects?
faq.answer: Skills are modular, distributable packages with standardized structure. Projects are broader workspace configurations. Skills work across platforms; projects are tool-specific.

name: skill-development
summary: This skill should be used when the user wants to "create a skill", "add a skill to plugin", "write a...
description: This skill should be used when the user wants to "create a skill", "add a skill to plugin", "write a new skill", "improve skill description", "organize skill content", or needs guidance on skill structure, progressive disclosure, or skill development best practices for Claude Code plugins.
author: anthropics
user_title: Create skills for Claude Code plugins
value_statement: Creating skills from scratch can lead to inconsistent structure and missed best practices. This skill provides a systematic six-step process for building effective skills with progressive disclosure, strong trigger descriptions, and proper resource organization.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through a six-step skill creation process
Explain skill anatomy: SKILL.md, scripts, references, and assets
Teach progressive disclosure for efficient context management
Provide writing style requirements: imperative form, third-person descriptions
Show validation checklist for quality assurance
Demonstrate skill structure with real examples from plugin-dev

use_cases.title: Build new plugin skills
use_cases.description: Create skills for your plugin following best practices for structure, triggers, and organization.

use_cases.title: Improve existing skills
use_cases.description: Refactor bloated SKILL.md files using progressive disclosure and move details to references.

use_cases.title: Design skill taxonomies
use_cases.description: Plan skill contents by analyzing use cases and identifying reusable scripts, references, and assets.

prompt_templates.title: Create new skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a skill for [TASK]. Walk me through the six-step skill creation process.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my existing skill at [PATH] and suggest improvements for trigger phrases, writing style, and progressive disclosure.

prompt_templates.title: Validate skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate my skill using the checklist. Check the description quality, body writing style, and resource organization.

prompt_templates.title: Structure guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain what goes in SKILL.md versus references/ versus scripts/ for my [SKILL TYPE] skill.

limitations: This skill focuses on plugin skills, not standalone skill packages
Does not include skill packaging or distribution scripts
Does not provide auto-generation of skill files from templates

faq.question: What is the difference between scripts, references, and assets?
faq.answer: Scripts contain executable code. References are documentation loaded as needed. Assets are files used in output like templates or images.

faq.question: What is the ideal length for SKILL.md?
faq.answer: Keep SKILL.md between 1,500-2,000 words. Move detailed content to references/ for progressive disclosure.

faq.question: How do skills work with Claude Code plugins?
faq.answer: Plugin skills live in the skills/ directory. Claude Code auto-discovers skills with SKILL.md files.

faq.question: Is user data stored when using this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance only. No data storage or transmission occurs.

faq.question: How is this skill different from the original skill-creator?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on plugin-specific skills with simpler manual structure instead of init_skill.py scripts.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for standalone skills?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for plugin skills distributed with Claude Code plugins, not standalone packages.

name: skill-installer
summary: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a u...
description: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos).
author: openai
value_statement: Finding and installing new skills for Codex requires navigating GitHub manually. This skill automates installation from curated lists or any GitHub repository, supporting both public and private repos with proper authentication.
actual_capabilities: List available curated skills from the OpenAI skills repository
Install skills from curated list by name
Install skills from any GitHub repository by owner/repo/path
Support private repositories using GitHub token authentication
Fallback to git sparse checkout when direct download fails
Validate skill structure and prevent overwriting existing installations

use_cases.title: Explore available skills
use_cases.description: Browse the curated skill library to discover new capabilities for your Codex installation.

use_cases.title: Install team skills
use_cases.description: Deploy custom skills from private team repositories across your organization.

use_cases.title: Add custom skills
use_cases.description: Install specialized skills from any public GitHub repository to extend Codex functionality.

prompt_templates.title: List available skills
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to see what skills are available
prompt_templates.prompt: What curated skills are available to install?

prompt_templates.title: Install by name
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to install a specific skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the skill-1 skill from the curated list.

prompt_templates.title: Install from GitHub
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to install from a specific repo
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the python-helper skill from the github.com/myorg/skills repository.

prompt_templates.title: Private repo install
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to install from a private repo
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the enterprise-skill from github.com/company/private-skills using my GITHUB_TOKEN.

limitations: Only supports GitHub as the skill source repository
Cannot install skills from local files or other version control systems
System skills are preinstalled and cannot be re-installed
GitHub API rate limits may affect listing performance

faq.question: Does this skill work with private GitHub repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. Set the GITHUB_TOKEN or GH_TOKEN environment variable to authenticate with private repos.

faq.question: Where are skills installed on my system?
faq.answer: Skills are installed to $CODEX_HOME/skills, which defaults to ~/.codex/skills.

faq.question: Can I integrate this skill with other automation tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --format json flag for machine-readable output suitable for scripts and pipelines.

faq.question: Is my code safe when installing skills from GitHub?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill validates paths and extracts archives safely to prevent directory traversal attacks.

faq.question: What should I do if installation fails?
faq.answer: Check that the repository exists, the path is correct, and you have proper authentication for private repos.

faq.question: How is this different from manual skill installation?
faq.answer: This skill automates the entire process including authentication handling, git fallback, and installation validation.
